So it's not about her eyes, it's about our eyes. And what it is, is that around her mouth, it's quite blurry. So Da Vinci painted it quite blurry and quite shadowy. And if you look at her eyes, then your peripheral vision is controlled by the rods in the edge of your eyes. So you've got rods and cones.

The rods are in the edge. And they're much better at making out shadows and there for night vision. So when you're looking in the Mona Lisa's eyes, the mouth looks like it's smiling because you see more of a blurry shadow there. But as soon as you look down to the mouth, the smile vanishes.
